name: Danish Apple Squares
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine flour and salt in a bowl.
2. Cut in shortening until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
3. In a separate bowl, beat egg yolk with enough milk to make 2/3 cup liquid.
4. Add the milk mixture to the flour mixture and stir until a dough forms.
5. Roll out half of the dough on a floured surface to fit a 12 x 10 inch baking pan.
6. Place the rolled dough in the pan, sprinkle with cornflakes, and arrange apple slices over the top.
7. Combine sugar and cinnamon; sprinkle over the remaining dough and apples.
8. Seal edges and cut slits in the top of the squares.
9. Beat egg white and brush over the tops.
10. Bake at 350°F for 50 minutes.
